steps.title: Identify the key alignment
steps.content: Henry's empirical finding—that companies received significant investment increases after liberalization—directly matches what the standard economic view predicted. This match is the decisive signal in the text.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states the relationship precisely: the empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus.

When Henry found companies receiving significant investment increases—exactly what economists predicted—it confirmed rather than contradicted their view.

The other three either introduce unsupported claims (negative effects, delayed benefits, country-specific comparisons) or contradict what the passage describes.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
